title: Application of Hessian Matrix in Optimization Decision Flow
description: A flowchart demonstrating how the Hessian matrix is used to classify critical points and guide optimization decisions.
relationships: Start at Function f(x) and find Critical Point
Calculate Hessian Matrix H(f) from f(x)
Evaluate H(f) at the Critical Point
Check the definiteness of the evaluated H(f)
If H(f) is Positive Definite → classify as Local Minimum
If H(f) is Negative Definite → classify as Local Maximum
If H(f) is Indefinite → classify as Saddle Point
If H(f) is semi-definite → Test Inconclusive; use higher-order tests
